context: Tests whether the agent can evaluate incoming code review comments, respond with agree/disagree reasoning for each, and implement the agreed-upon changes — following the rails-review-response skill's structured response format.

checklist.name: Review response document created
checklist.description: A docs/review_response.md file is produced that addresses all 4 review comments

checklist.name: Comment 1 implemented correctly
checklist.description: update? now checks that the current user is the project owner (record.user == user) OR a team admin — not just user.present?

checklist.name: Comment 2 refactoring applied
checklist.description: A private method (owner_or_admin? or similar) is extracted and both update? and destroy? delegate to it

checklist.name: Comment 3 YARD added
checklist.description: Public policy methods (index?, show?, create?, update?, destroy?) have YARD @return [Boolean] documentation

checklist.name: Comment 4 addressed in response
checklist.description: The review response explains why scope.all does not filter by active: true (either intentional with explanation, or a fix is applied)

checklist.name: Agree/disagree labels in response
checklist.description: Each comment in docs/review_response.md is labeled Agree, Disagree, or Acknowledged with clear reasoning

checklist.name: frozen_string_literal preserved
checklist.description: The updated policy file still has # frozen_string_literal: true

checklist.name: No unrelated changes
checklist.description: The policy file has no changes beyond what the review comments requested (index?, show?, create? are unchanged)

checklist.name: owner_or_admin? logic is correct
checklist.description: The private method checks both conditions correctly — record ownership AND admin role — using OR, not AND

checklist.name: destroy? uses same logic as update?
checklist.description: destroy? delegates to owner_or_admin? (or the same logic as update?) after comment 2 is applied

checklist.name: Response is professional
checklist.description: The review response is written in a respectful, constructive tone consistent with the rails-review-response skill expectations

checklist.name: Scope class preserved
checklist.description: The Scope::resolve method is either kept as-is (with an explanatory comment) or updated with a clear rationale
